column1: Sentence
PROJECT PURPOSE
Automation of testing files for antivirus detections (AV in the future), and client correctness.
It is supposed to work in the following environment:
* OS:
Windows 10 with the latest updates
* AV: Windows Defender (built-in AV of 10).
Only this AB is of interest!
* there is a cryptopanel - it is a site where samples for testing are located.
There is a connection to it through normal web requests.
* there is an admin panel - this is a site, which is contacted by the client at startup, and writes logs of its work there.
MANUAL TESTING SCENARIO
1.  Preparing the virtual machine
2.  Uploading the archive with the file from the cryptopanel to the desktop of the virtual machine
3.  Checking the AB response to the archive
4.  Unpacking the archive
5.  Checking the AB response on the unpacked file(s)
6.  Running the unpacked file
7.  Checking the AB operation on the launched file
Waiting for the specified time
9.  Check if any of the directories (hereinafter referred to as %dir%) has been created:
%appdata%\vcmsd\.
default user\%appdata%\vcmcsd
(see parameter #8 in the REALIZATION OPTIONS section - the check goes through this list)
Getting client ID from %dir%\FAQ file
Connection to the admin
waiting for a specified time
Searching for program tap by received client ID
Knockback represents several events:
appearance of a NEW record with such client ID in the admin area.
(the fact of a non-empty response from the admin API, and the presence in it of a record of the form
appearance of a record about system info module launch (presence in the response from the admin API of a record of the following type
appearance of the record about injectDll module start (presence in the response from the admin API of a record of the following type
injectDll 2018-08-30 11:20:26.490341 start Success
Check of AB operation after knockback
At ANY DETECT, further testing should stop.
All subsequent tests should be given the status SKIPPED.
SKIPPED actually means a test failure, but we introduce a separate status to distinguish it from a simple failure.
A failure notification (sample name, list of tests with results) should be sent to the crypto panel.
AUTOTESTS
An autotest is a subroutine that checks one simple condition and returns TRUE/FALSE if the condition is met.
The list of autotests for this scenario is:
1. archive_static_detect - whether AB was detected in step 3
2. unarchived_static_detect - whether AB was detected at step 5
3. proactive_detect - was AB detected at step 7
4. client_installed - whether the client was installed (step 10)
5. client_knocked - whether the client has knocked to the admin (step 13.1)
6. client_sysinfo_loaded - whether the client has knocked into the admin area step 13.2
7. client_inject_loaded - whether the client has tapped into the admin area step 13.3
8. client_modules_detect - whether AB was detected at step 14.
IMPLEMENTATION OPTIONS
It is suggested to implement autotests on Powershell if possible v2.0.
The choice of the version is due to the fact that v2.0 is available on all OS starting from Windows 7.
Access to the cryptopanel is supposed to be via API (not ready yet).
A mandatory parameter when working through the API
will be the network name of the computer.
API ADMINKA
https://185.64.105.33/du8ASnIODJjksdb89fsibndg7s6giosulDSGsdgsyb78s87dgdsgszklj8zgh8dsgisgsdg/clientID_suffix
Here clientID_suffix is the part of the string from the FAQ file after the dot.
For example, if the file C:/users/user/AppData/Roaming/vsmcd/FAQ contains the line DESKTOP-4KGAGIP_W10014393.701EBF802F2CB023A6C24D34D9F31071
then clientID_suffix is the string 701EBF802F2CB023A6C24D34D9F31071
The request should be sent via HTTP POST
The request can contain the following fields:
- pass: password.
The value is always fixed - string f779f60d4868063d462c3f99656a8a6d
- timeout: the depth of the search time, in minutes.
The parameter is optional, the default value on the server is 15 minutes.
It is recommended to set the timeout time equal to "now", the "start of the tap search timeout".
For example
- tap check started at 13:02
- at 13:07 we do a tap check, we calculate the timeout value to be set in the request: 13:07-13:02 == 5.
The server responds that there is no data (there was no tap).
- at 13:08 we check the tap again, calculate the timeout value to be set in the request: 13:08-13:02 == 6.
The server responds that there is no data (there was no tap).
The server responds that there is no data (there was no tap).
- at 13:09 we check the tap again, calculate the timeout value to be set in the request: 13:09-13:02 == 7.
The server sends data indicating that there was a tap.
We finish the test with the status "success".
The presence of the timeout parameter is caused by the fact that autotests on one and the same machine may occur frequently (every 15-20 minutes).
At the same time a machine may have the same clientID.
If you do not filter the data by time, the server may give the
tapping data from the previous test (for example, we tested 20 minutes ago on the same machine, and the server gave us tapping data from the previous test).
from the previous test -- this is wrong; the expected result is that there was no tapping for the current test).
CRYPTOPANEL API
The crypto panel provides an API with the following operations:
1. give me the next highest priority file in the queue to check on the machine named %MACHINE%
HTTP POST /some-random-and-long-api-prefix/getfile
Parameters:
pass: shardcoded password - common to the entire API.
String.
machine: domain name of the computer.
String.
This name is taken directly from the computer properties in the OS.
Answer: json of the following form:
"file_id": 32, // file identifier in the database
{ "file": "TVqQAAMAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA//8AA" // base64 encoded file body
Starting from the moment the file is given, this machine is marked in the database as checking this file.
Repeated requests from the same machine are considered valid --
this means a network failure when receiving the file on the previous request.
2. take the results of file verification on the %MACHINE% machine
HTTP POST /some-random-and-long-api-prefix/setresult
Parameters:
pass: shardcoded password - common to the entire API.
String.
machine: domain name of the computer.
String.
status: 0|1. number.
Only two values are possible - denoting "ok" and "failed" respectively.
Indicates the overall result of the test.
log: test log.
A long string (up to 64k, but in theory it can be more) in base64 encoding.
file_id: number.
The identifier of the file obtained in the previous request.
The system looks to see which file was checked on the given machine and gives it the appropriate status.
IF the file has NOT been checked on the given machine, or the time of check start is too old (more than 24 hours), this request should be ignored as invalid.
as invalid.
SCRIPT CONFIGURATION
The script should be easily configurable by someone who does not know PowerShell.
For this purpose, the following parameters should be included in the script header
the following parameters should be placed in the script header (the list is not exclusive - other parameters can be placed there as needed):
1. URL of the cryptopanel for downloading the archive
2. cryptopanel login
3.  Cryptopanel password
4. URL of the admin area
5.  Admin login
6.  Admin password
7.  Test result check periodicity, in seconds (sleep_every)
8.  List of folder names to check if the installation was successful
OR URLs in the crypto panel, where to get their list from
Waiting timeout from completion of archive download to static detection result 1 (in seconds) (1 minute)
Timeout from unpacking the archive to the result of static detection 2 (in seconds) (1 minute)
waiting timeout from client startup to proactive detection result (in seconds) (5 minutes)
waiting timeout from client startup to check the creation of client working directories (in seconds) (5 minutes)
Waiting timeout from client startup to verify client bounces to the admin area (in seconds) (15 minutes)
(probably there will be several timeouts, according to the number of sub-steps 13.1, 13.2, etc. - at the programmer's discretion)
sysInfo module - 10 minutes
injectDll module - 5 minutes
Waiting timeout from the client's knockback to the admin, to the result of proactive detection (steps 13-14) (in seconds) (2 minutes).
Regarding timeouts -- the point is that we wait a certain amount of time for each event.
If we haven't waited for an event (minutes)
then we decide on the outcome of the test.
So we should have a timeout for each event, even if it is not mentioned in the ToR.
The algorithm of all tests is as follows (C-style pseudocode):
bool result = false;
time_t started = time(NULL);
while(time(NULL) - started < test_timeout) {
if(test condition) {
result = true;
break;
sleep(sleep_every);
time_t end = time(NULL);
That is, we loop in a loop, waking up every sleep_every second, until the expected condition (e.g., a detection or a knock) occurs.
If the condition has not occurred in test_timeout, we terminate the test and publish the test result.
For detects, the logic is inverse
true is a Failure of the test (there was a detect :-(
false is test success (there was no detection)
It is suggested to check Windows Defender detections by logs in Event Viewer\Applications and Services Logs\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Defender
This can be done with a commandlet (see https://letitknow.wordpress.com/2012/09/02/powershell-and-the-applications-and-services-logs/):
Get-WinEvent "Microsoft-Windows-Windows Defender/Operational"
TEST RESULTS
Display test results with the start and end times labeled on the screen:
2018-08-14 00:01:01:02 archive_static_detect started
2018-08-14 00:01:01:12 archive_static_detect:
OK
2018-08-14 00:01:01:22 unarchived_static_detect started
OK
2018-08-14 00:01:01:42 proactive_detect started
2018-08-14 00:01:01:52 proactive_detect: FAILED!
2018-08-14 00:02:02:02 client_installed started
2018-08-14 00:01:22 client_knocked started
2018-08-14 00:01:32 client_knocked: SKIPPED
2018-08-14 00:01:01:32 client_sysinfo_loaded started
2018-08-14 00:01:01:32 client_sysinfo_loaded: SKIPPED
2018-08-14 00:01:01:32 client_modules_detect: SKIPPED
The script should return a value equal to the number of failed tests to the OS.
The script should output the result of the tests (full log and overall success/failure status) to the crypto panel
SCRIPT AUTO-UPDATE
Full test automation is possible only with automation of virtual machine preparation for the test.
Preparation for the test includes:
- updating Windows with all the latest changes (this is done once a day from the start and cannot be automated)
- auto-updating the autotest script.
The script will be constantly updated, because the working directories of the binary being tested will change.
It is suggested to update the autotest script via GitLab API.
This requires 2 HTTP requests:
- to authorize in GitLab
- to upload the script.
A detailed Unix shell login script is given here:
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/47948887/login-to-gitlab-using-curl
Accordingly, the following script configuration parameters should be added:
- GitLab web interface URL
- GitLab credentials
- URI to the script source on GitLab.
